intro: Stenostomum oliganthum, also known as "wild jasmine" or "Mexican jasmine," is a flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, characterized by its fragrant, star-shaped flowers and slender foliage. Traditionally, Stenostomum oliganthum has been used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality. The bioactive constituents of Stenostomum oliganthum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and essential oils, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Stenostomum oliganthum is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often used to calm the nervous system and promote emotional well-being.
